message:
fix(e2e): force `cargo run` to execute locally instead of via rch

Add a cargo_command_requires_local_execution() guard that detects when
the cargo subcommand is "run" and bypasses the rch remote execution
path. This fixes the e2e smoke tests when FFS_USE_RCH=1 is set, because
`cargo run` needs to execute locally (it launches the ffs binary which
must access the local filesystem and FUSE mount point). Remote execution
of `cargo run` via rch would fail or produce incorrect results since the
binary would run on the remote build host rather than the test machine.

The guard is applied consistently across all three cargo wrapper
functions: run_cargo_assert(), run_cargo(), and capture_cargo_output().
Other cargo commands (build, test, check, etc.) continue to use rch
when available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
